mapping.a_element: low-energy effective actions parameterizing interactions of particle-physics candidates with optical photons and external electromagnetic fields
mapping.b_element: effective field theory methods for the dilute Bose gas, parameterizing interactions via the gas parameter √(na³)

mapping.a_element: external electromagnetic fields as probes to extract microscopic properties of new physics
mapping.b_element: scattering length and effective range as probes to extract microscopic properties of the interatomic potential

mapping.a_element: perturbative characterization of optical signatures in quantum vacuum experiments
mapping.b_element: perturbative expansion of properties (e.g., ground state energy density) in the gas parameter √(na³)

brief_justification: While both papers employ low-energy effective field theory frameworks and perturbative expansions, Paper A focuses on quantum vacuum experiments probing new physics via external fields, whereas Paper B addresses equilibrium properties of a Bose gas using field-theoretic techniques like Bogoliubov approximations and renormalization group methods. The core machinery—quantum field interactions in external fields vs. many-body quantum statistics—differs fundamentally.
